title: aquilegia pyrenaica var. bertolonii
intro: Aquilegia pyrenaica var. bertolonii, also known as the Pyrenean columbine or Bertoloni's columbine, is a plant species within the genus Aquilegia, characterized by its delicate, bell-shaped flowers in shades of pink and white. The plant's roots and flowers are traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Research suggests that Aquilegia pyrenaica var. bertolonii may offer health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting respiratory health. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often combined with other herbs to create teas, infusions, or topical applications to leverage its medicinal properties.
